reforms.description: In 2002, Germany introduced disease management programs (DMP) aimed at improving chronic disease care by establishing care standards and encouraging better integration of care for chronically ill patients.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The DMP aims to improve healthcare outcomes, which can lead to increased productivity and reduced healthcare costs.
reforms.status_evidence: The DMP has been fully operational since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of DMP represents a significant policy change in healthcare management.
reforms.source_quote: A major reform effort was the introduction of so-called disease management programs (DMP) for chronic disease care in 2002.

reforms.description: Germany has implemented measures to foster venture capital, reduce regulatory barriers, and provide public financial support for high technology-oriented SMEs, including accelerated depreciation allowances for newly-founded enterprises and the 'Venture Capital for small technology companies' (BTU) programme.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures enhance the availability of funding for innovative startups, which can drive economic growth and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been fully operational since their introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The comprehensive support for startups and venture capital represents a major shift in policy to stimulate innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The main federal government programme to promote venture capital for particularly high technology orientated small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) is entitled 'Venture Capital for small technology companies' or BTU.
